Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 64.2 mph
swing length 6.4 ft
attack angle 3.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.7+3.8*2-1.0on plane
Cutter-6.2+3.8*2-2.4on plane
Curveball-10.1+3.8*0-6.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sinker-5.5+3.8*0-1.8on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Jesús SánchezLHBTOR vs Brenan Hanifee (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.522 / xBA 0.361 / xSLG 0.764 (+207 pts)K 10.1%BB 7.7%HR 4.9%Hit 41.8%Strong Hitter

Jesús Sánchez LHB vs Brenan Hanifee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 517+1942+2002, pitcher rows 112+966+481

Verdict

Projection: 0.522 xwOBA (+207 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +24 pts (raw 0.464 -> adj 0.488); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +34 pts (bb_type-adj 0.488 -> final 0.522); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jesús Sánchez is on a 14-day slump (-72 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch Out0.20.3450.393GB 33% / LD 27% / FB 33% / PU 7%0.376+310.4540.3440.73112.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

3.Juan SotoLHBNYM vs Cam Schlittler (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.495 / xBA 0.334 / xSLG 0.728 (+180 pts)K 21.5%BB 16.4%HR 9.8%Hit 28.0%Strong Hitter

Juan Soto LHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 441+3015+3255, pitcher rows 792+1448+0

Verdict

Projection: 0.495 xwOBA (+180 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.481 -> final 0.495); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Juan Soto is on a 14-day slump (-139 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

4.Brandon ValenzuelaLHBTOR vs Brenan Hanifee (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.491 / xBA 0.351 / xSLG 0.583 (+176 pts)K 10.3%BB 10.1%HR 6.3%Hit 39.5%Strong Hitter

Brandon Valenzuela LHB vs Brenan Hanifee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 301+0+0, pitcher rows 112+966+481

Verdict

Hanifee's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Valenzuela should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.491 xwOBA (+176 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.418 -> adj 0.428); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +63 pts (bb_type-adj 0.428 -> final 0.491); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brandon Valenzuela is on a 14-day heater (+119 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

5.Ketel MarteLHBAZ vs Kyle Freeland (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.484 / xBA 0.367 / xSLG 0.704 (+169 pts)K 12.9%BB 4.2%HR 7.0%Hit 36.2%Strong Hitter

Ketel Marte LHB vs Kyle Freeland LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 602+2117+2175, pitcher rows 514+2490+1769

Verdict

Projection: 0.484 xwOBA (+169 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +31 pts (raw 0.438 -> adj 0.469); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.469 -> final 0.484); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ketel Marte is on a 14-day slump (-71 pts vs season). Kyle Freeland is in a 14-day rough patch (+59 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Projection: 0.473 xwOBA (+158 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.462 -> final 0.473); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. 8.Sam AntonacciLHBCWS vs Edward Cabrera (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.472 / xBA 0.435 / xSLG 0.625 (+157 pts)K 9.0%BB 7.9%HR 0.9%Hit 35.6%Strong Hitter

Sam Antonacci LHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 388+0+0, pitcher rows 725+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Sam Antonacci should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.472 xwOBA (+157 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.501 -> adj 0.475); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Edward Cabrera is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 9.Josh BellRHBMIN vs Coleman Crow (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.465 / xBA 0.449 / xSLG 0.616 (+150 pts)K 28.1%BB 0.0%HR 5.9%Hit 42.5%Strong Hitter

Josh Bell RHB vs Coleman Crow RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 664+2103+2242, pitcher rows 77+0+0

Verdict

Crow's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Josh Bell should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.465 xwOBA (+150 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +19 pts (raw 0.448 -> adj 0.467); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Josh Bell is on a 14-day slump (-93 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

11.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Kyle Freeland (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.457 / xBA 0.341 / xSLG 0.702 (+142 pts)K 19.2%BB 5.4%HR 6.9%Hit 34.1%Strong Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Kyle Freeland LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 660+2514+2649, pitcher rows 514+2490+1769

Verdict

Freeland's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.457 xwOBA (+142 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.435 -> adj 0.446); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +11 pts (bb_type-adj 0.446 -> final 0.457); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Freeland is in a 14-day rough patch (+59 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

12.Royce LewisRHBMIN vs Coleman Crow (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.456 / xBA 0.464 / xSLG 0.580 (+141 pts)K 34.2%BB 0.0%HR 5.3%Hit 40.5%Strong Hitter

Royce Lewis RHB vs Coleman Crow RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 418+1518+1242, pitcher rows 77+0+0

Verdict

Crow's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Royce Lewis should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.456 xwOBA (+141 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.435 -> adj 0.446); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.446 -> final 0.456); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Royce Lewis is on a 14-day slump (-49 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

13.Yordan AlvarezLHBHOU vs Jack Leiter (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.449 / xBA 0.296 / xSLG 0.620 (+134 pts)K 16.4%BB 14.1%HR 6.8%Hit 28.3%Strong Hitter

Yordan Alvarez LHB vs Jack Leiter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 768+824+2359, pitcher rows 749+2585+669

Verdict

Projection: 0.449 xwOBA (+134 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.459 -> adj 0.425); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.425 -> final 0.449); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Yordan Alvarez is on a 14-day slump (-82 pts vs season). Jack Leiter is in a 14-day hot stretch (-63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

15.Jac CaglianoneLHBKC vs Dustin May (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.445 / xBA 0.298 / xSLG 0.608 (+130 pts)K 24.9%BB 11.0%HR 7.0%Hit 30.9%Strong Hitter

Jac Caglianone LHB vs Dustin May RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 528+858+0, pitcher rows 690+2182+0

Verdict

May's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jac Caglianone should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.445 xwOBA (+130 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.418 -> adj 0.423); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.423 -> final 0.445); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dustin May is in a 14-day rough patch (+60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

16.Jonathan ArandaLHBTB vs Janson Junk (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.441 / xBA 0.331 / xSLG 0.584 (+126 pts)K 17.1%BB 5.3%HR 6.7%Hit 33.5%Strong Hitter

Jonathan Aranda LHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 728+1732+574, pitcher rows 677+1622+175

Verdict

Projection: 0.441 xwOBA (+126 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -32 pts (raw 0.442 -> adj 0.411); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.411 -> final 0.441); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jonathan Aranda is on a 14-day heater (+51 pts vs season). Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

17.Luke KeaschallRHBMIN vs Coleman Crow (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.441 / xBA 0.448 / xSLG 0.559 (+126 pts)K 21.6%BB 0.0%HR 2.3%Hit 42.6%Strong Hitter

Luke Keaschall RHB vs Coleman Crow RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 687+775+0, pitcher rows 77+0+0

Verdict

Crow's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Luke Keaschall should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.441 xwOBA (+126 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.427 -> adj 0.450); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.450 -> final 0.441); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

18.Jackson MerrillLHBSD vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.441 / xBA 0.302 / xSLG 0.621 (+126 pts)K 19.1%BB 7.3%HR 5.7%Hit 33.0%Strong Hitter

Jackson Merrill LHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 692+1748+2291, pitcher rows 741+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Jackson Merrill should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.441 xwOBA (+126 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.420 -> adj 0.408); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +33 pts (bb_type-adj 0.408 -> final 0.441); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 70.9 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 8.1°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+4.0264-0.5on plane
Sinker-5.7+1.066-4.7swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.5+14.998+7.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-7.1+6.679-0.5on plane
Sweeper-7.9+14.933+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-8.2+12.463+4.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-9.8+12.734+2.8on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Verdict

Hanifee's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Daulton Varsho should look to drive the changeup. Projection: 0.440 xwOBA (+125 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.388 -> adj 0.406); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +34 pts (bb_type-adj 0.406 -> final 0.440); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

20.Alejandro OsunaLHBTEX vs Spencer Arrighetti (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.439 / xBA 0.330 / xSLG 0.526 (+124 pts)K 22.1%BB 20.0%HR 2.1%Hit 26.7%Strong Hitter

Alejandro Osuna LHB vs Spencer Arrighetti RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 182+744+0, pitcher rows 472+619+2509

Verdict

Arrighetti's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Alejandro Osuna should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.439 xwOBA (+124 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.443 -> adj 0.451); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.451 -> final 0.439); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Alejandro Osuna is on a 14-day slump (-60 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

21.James OutmanLHBMIN vs Coleman Crow (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.438 / xBA 0.215 / xSLG 0.461 (+123 pts)K 16.4%BB 7.9%HR 6.6%Hit 28.2%Strong Hitter

James Outman LHB vs Coleman Crow RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 151+605+601, pitcher rows 77+0+0

Verdict

Crow's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while James Outman should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.438 xwOBA (+123 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.345 -> adj 0.361); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +76 pts (bb_type-adj 0.361 -> final 0.438); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

22.Oneil CruzLHBPIT vs Aaron Nola (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.436 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.544 (+121 pts)K 28.8%BB 9.7%HR 3.7%Hit 33.0%Strong Hitter

Oneil Cruz LHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 764+2250+2404, pitcher rows 734+1599+3280

Verdict

Nola's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Oneil Cruz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.436 xwOBA (+121 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +20 pts (raw 0.367 -> adj 0.386); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +50 pts (bb_type-adj 0.386 -> final 0.436); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Nola is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

4-Seam Fastball33.00.3840.364GB 22% / LD 21% / FB 42% / PU 14%0.406+230.4550.2690.74910.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

24.Shea LangeliersRHBATH vs Tyler Mahle (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.434 / xBA 0.328 / xSLG 0.593 (+119 pts)K 15.5%BB 10.0%HR 8.2%Hit 27.8%Strong Hitter

Shea Langeliers RHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 671+1930+2003, pitcher rows 716+1381+229

Verdict

Projection: 0.434 xwOBA (+119 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.411 -> adj 0.421); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.421 -> final 0.434); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shea Langeliers is on a 14-day heater (+97 pts vs season). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-75 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

25.Luke RaleyLHBSEA vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.433 / xBA 0.280 / xSLG 0.600 (+118 pts)K 20.2%BB 8.0%HR 7.0%Hit 28.1%Strong Hitter

Luke Raley LHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 479+850+1714, pitcher rows 723+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Luke Raley should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.433 xwOBA (+118 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +12 pts (raw 0.394 -> adj 0.405); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.405 -> final 0.433); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Luke Raley is on a 14-day heater (+74 pts vs season). Randy Vásquez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

26.Josh NaylorLHBSEA vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.433 / xBA 0.339 / xSLG 0.604 (+118 pts)K 8.2%BB 9.4%HR 4.4%Hit 32.2%Strong Hitter

Josh Naylor LHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 597+2196+2463, pitcher rows 723+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Josh Naylor should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.433 xwOBA (+118 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.419 -> adj 0.428); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.428 -> final 0.433); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Randy Vásquez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball29.10.3950.373GB 35% / LD 23% / FB 34% / PU 8%0.389-60.4460.3420.6354.6
Cutter22.60.4100.383GB 34% / LD 28% / FB 26% / PU 12%0.443+330.5100.4210.7263.4
Curveball19.10.3580.324GB 48% / LD 19% / FB 28% / PU 6%0.329-290.3380.2550.46623.5
Changeup15.00.3490.372GB 40% / LD 33% / FB 18% / PU 8%0.388+390.4440.3920.65421.1
Sweeper6.90.2650.335GB 35% / LD 7% / FB 36% / PU 21%0.232-330.2520.1460.35114.9
Sinker6.20.3510.406GB 32% / LD 38% / FB 26% / PU 4%0.435+840.5260.4090.6844.3
Slider1.20.3470.111GB 37% / LD 25% / FB 29% / PU 10%0.338-100.1340.1140.19719.5

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

27.Rafael DeversLHBSF vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.432 / xBA 0.287 / xSLG 0.582 (+117 pts)K 24.9%BB 13.1%HR 5.7%Hit 28.5%Strong Hitter

Rafael Devers LHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 729+3102+2433, pitcher rows 719+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.432 xwOBA (+117 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +19 pts (raw 0.398 -> adj 0.417); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.417 -> final 0.432); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

28.Gabriel MorenoRHBAZ vs Kyle Freeland (LHP)proj xwOBA 0.429 / xBA 0.383 / xSLG 0.604 (+114 pts)K 11.6%BB 4.9%HR 2.8%Hit 38.7%Strong Hitter

Gabriel Moreno RHB vs Kyle Freeland LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 334+1324+1538, pitcher rows 514+2490+1769

Verdict

Projection: 0.429 xwOBA (+114 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.429 -> adj 0.439); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.439 -> final 0.429); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Freeland is in a 14-day rough patch (+59 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

30.Aaron JudgeRHBNYY vs Clay Holmes (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.427 / xBA 0.301 / xSLG 0.544 (+112 pts)K 24.5%BB 11.5%HR 6.3%Hit 27.1%Strong Hitter

Aaron Judge RHB vs Clay Holmes RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 832+2875+3235, pitcher rows 733+2766+1260

Verdict

Holmes' curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Aaron Judge should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.427 xwOBA (+112 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -61 pts (raw 0.454 -> adj 0.394); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +33 pts (bb_type-adj 0.394 -> final 0.427); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-71 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

31.Mickey MoniakLHBCOL vs Merrill Kelly (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.427 / xBA 0.303 / xSLG 0.643 (+112 pts)K 18.9%BB 5.4%HR 7.5%Hit 30.6%Strong Hitter

Mickey Moniak LHB vs Merrill Kelly RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 509+1602+1534, pitcher rows 483+2835+1143

Verdict

Kelly's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mickey Moniak should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.427 xwOBA (+112 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +25 pts (bb_type-adj 0.402 -> final 0.427); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mickey Moniak is on a 14-day heater (+41 pts vs season). Merrill Kelly is in a 14-day hot stretch (-88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

32.Gavin SheetsLHBSD vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.425 / xBA 0.297 / xSLG 0.591 (+110 pts)K 17.6%BB 9.3%HR 5.3%Hit 30.5%Strong Hitter

Gavin Sheets LHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 524+2301+2094, pitcher rows 741+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Gavin Sheets should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.425 xwOBA (+110 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.405 -> final 0.425); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Michael Busch LHB vs Sean Burke RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 723+2419+2369, pitcher rows 699+2380+309

Verdict

Projection: 0.423 xwOBA (+108 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.417 -> adj 0.409); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.409 -> final 0.423); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+93 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

34.Brandon LoweLHBPIT vs Aaron Nola (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.423 / xBA 0.304 / xSLG 0.575 (+108 pts)K 22.9%BB 9.3%HR 5.9%Hit 29.7%Strong Hitter

Brandon Lowe LHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 710+2148+1632, pitcher rows 734+1599+3280

Verdict

Nola's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Brandon Lowe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.423 xwOBA (+108 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.404 -> adj 0.396); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.396 -> final 0.423); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Nola is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

35.Pete AlonsoRHBBAL vs Zack Littell (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.423 / xBA 0.290 / xSLG 0.602 (+108 pts)K 17.2%BB 4.5%HR 6.5%Hit 30.8%Strong Hitter

Pete Alonso RHB vs Zack Littell RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 745+2825+3049, pitcher rows 583+2843+2488

Verdict

Littell's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Alonso should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.423 xwOBA (+108 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +27 pts (bb_type-adj 0.396 -> final 0.423); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Pete Alonso is on a 14-day heater (+53 pts vs season). Zack Littell is in a 14-day hot stretch (-64 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider31.20.3430.391GB 44% / LD 28% / FB 24% / PU 5%0.356+130.4320.3260.64726.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

36.Brent RookerRHBATH vs Tyler Mahle (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.423 / xBA 0.312 / xSLG 0.559 (+108 pts)K 18.6%BB 11.6%HR 8.0%Hit 25.9%Strong Hitter

Brent Rooker RHB vs Tyler Mahle RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 407+2590+2391, pitcher rows 716+1381+229

Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Brent Rooker should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.423 xwOBA (+108 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.424 -> adj 0.402); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.402 -> final 0.423); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brent Rooker is on a 14-day heater (+58 pts vs season). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-75 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

37.Dominic CanzoneLHBSEA vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.421 / xBA 0.298 / xSLG 0.571 (+106 pts)K 15.2%BB 7.8%HR 3.7%Hit 32.6%Strong Hitter

Dominic Canzone LHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 382+1134+704, pitcher rows 723+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Dominic Canzone should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.421 xwOBA (+106 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.406 -> adj 0.394); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.394 -> final 0.421); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Randy Vásquez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

38.Austin MartinRHBMIN vs Coleman Crow (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.419 / xBA 0.445 / xSLG 0.532 (+104 pts)K 25.9%BB 0.0%HR 0.1%Hit 43.8%Strong Hitter

Austin Martin RHB vs Coleman Crow RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 533+749+934, pitcher rows 77+0+0

Verdict

Crow's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Martin should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.419 xwOBA (+104 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.424 -> adj 0.432); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.432 -> final 0.419); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

39.Brendan DonovanLHBSEA vs Randy Vásquez (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.417 / xBA 0.333 / xSLG 0.574 (+102 pts)K 6.7%BB 9.5%HR 3.2%Hit 30.7%Strong Hitter

Brendan Donovan LHB vs Randy Vásquez RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 323+1933+2487, pitcher rows 723+2153+1692

Verdict

Vásquez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brendan Donovan should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.417 xwOBA (+102 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.428 -> adj 0.416); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Randy Vásquez is in a 14-day hot stretch (-45 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Crow's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Brooks Lee should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.413 xwOBA (+98 pts vs league, Hitter). 41.Jake McCarthyLHBCOL vs Merrill Kelly (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.410 / xBA 0.322 / xSLG 0.612 (+95 pts)K 12.8%BB 7.0%HR 2.9%Hit 32.5%Edge Hitter

Jake McCarthy LHB vs Merrill Kelly RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 334+846+1792, pitcher rows 483+2835+1143

Verdict

Kelly's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake McCarthy should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.410 xwOBA (+95 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +38 pts (raw 0.366 -> adj 0.404); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.404 -> final 0.410); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake McCarthy is on a 14-day heater (+84 pts vs season). Merrill Kelly is in a 14-day hot stretch (-88 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Arrighetti's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Joc Pederson should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.409 xwOBA (+94 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.384 -> adj 0.393); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.393 -> final 0.409); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joc Pederson is on a 14-day heater (+49 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

43.Carter JensenLHBKC vs Dustin May (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.408 / xBA 0.266 / xSLG 0.479 (+93 pts)K 30.8%BB 16.6%HR 5.9%Hit 24.4%Edge Hitter

Carter Jensen LHB vs Dustin May RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 608+293+0, pitcher rows 690+2182+0

Verdict

May's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Carter Jensen should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.408 xwOBA (+93 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.387 -> adj 0.369); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +40 pts (bb_type-adj 0.369 -> final 0.408); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carter Jensen is on a 14-day slump (-47 pts vs season). Dustin May is in a 14-day rough patch (+60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

May's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bobby Witt Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.408 xwOBA (+93 pts vs league, Hitter). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

45.Yohendrick PiñangoLHBTOR vs Brenan Hanifee (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.406 / xBA 0.409 / xSLG 0.621 (+91 pts)K 3.9%BB 10.0%HR 0.0%Hit 38.2%Edge Hitter

Yohendrick Piñango LHB vs Brenan Hanifee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 140+0+0, pitcher rows 112+966+481

Verdict

Hanifee's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Yohendrick Piñango should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.406 xwOBA (+91 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.431 -> adj 0.441); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -35 pts (bb_type-adj 0.441 -> final 0.406); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

46.Jarred KelenicLHBCWS vs Edward Cabrera (RHP)proj xwOBA 0.406 / xBA 0.278 / xSLG 0.523 (+91 pts)K 28.9%BB 11.1%HR 4.0%Hit 28.6%Edge Hitter

Jarred Kelenic LHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 135+258+1762, pitcher rows 725+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jarred Kelenic should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.406 xwOBA (+91 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.369 -> adj 0.374); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.374 -> final 0.406); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Edward Cabrera is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

47.Fernando Tatis Jr.RHBSD vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.406 / xBA 0.309 / xSLG 0.556 (+91 pts)K 16.2%BB 6.0%HR 4.5%Hit 31.4%Edge Hitter

Fernando Tatis Jr. RHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 674+2699+1628, pitcher rows 741+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Fernando Tatis Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.406 xwOBA (+91 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.387 -> adj 0.395); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.395 -> final 0.406); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 29+52+0, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Projection: 0.404 xwOBA (+89 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.414 -> adj 0.404); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

50.Gunnar HendersonLHBBAL vs Zack Littell (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.404 / xBA 0.292 / xSLG 0.575 (+89 pts)K 15.9%BB 5.6%HR 5.2%Hit 31.0%Edge Hitter

Gunnar Henderson LHB vs Zack Littell RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 772+2528+2944, pitcher rows 583+2843+2488

Verdict

Littell's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Gunnar Henderson should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.404 xwOBA (+89 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.383 -> final 0.404); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Gunnar Henderson is on a 14-day slump (-81 pts vs season). Zack Littell is in a 14-day hot stretch (-64 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Pitcherlast 14d————————
Pitcherlast 30d570.4540.3490.7093.58.85.346.0

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider30.90.3810.359GB 31% / LD 23% / FB 34% / PU 13%0.365-170.4080.3010.64321.8
Split-Finger29.50.3320.293GB 48% / LD 26% / FB 22% / PU 4%0.341+80.3180.2950.46030.1
4-Seam Fastball27.60.3850.349GB 30% / LD 25% / FB 34% / PU 12%0.366-190.4000.2810.58610.5
Sinker9.50.3740.381GB 31% / LD 19% / FB 41% / PU 9%0.411+370.4770.3210.7264.9
Sweeper2.50.3580.234GB 37% / LD 24% / FB 30% / PU 9%0.357-00.2760.1670.38620.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

PitchIn-zone %Top zones (attack share)Intersection xwOBAIntersection xBAIntersection xSLGCoverage %

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 74.2 mph
swing length 7.3 ft
attack angle 8.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Split-Finger-8.6+13.947+5.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-6.5+13.572+7.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.9+2.6244-2.3on plane
Sinker-5.5+4.094-1.5on plane
Sweeper-9.0+16.234+7.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

  • Hitters are ranked across every posted (or projected) lineup for the day, by projected xwOBA against their listed opposing starter.
